items.subject: Kimi K3, and what we can still learn from the pelican benchmark
items.reason: Simon Willison's detailed analysis of Moonshot AI's Kimi K3 (2.8T params). Covers benchmarks, pricing ($3/$15 per M tokens), pelican SVG test, Arena.ai frontend code #1, comparison to Claude Fable 5 / GPT-5.6 Sol. Major model release from tracked opinion leader.
items.action: Create entity page for Kimi K3 / Moonshot AI; update benchmark concepts; enrich Simon Willison page

items.subject: China's Open Source K3 Changed the Game Forever
items.reason: Beehiiv newsletter covering Kimi K3 open-source release. All links are beehiiv tracking redirects — could not resolve to actual article URLs without following redirects. Overlaps with Simon Willison coverage. Pair both for comprehensive K3 entity page.
items.action: Merge with Simon Willison K3 coverage; supplementary angle on Chinese open-source landscape

items.subject: Building an Enterprise AI Agent for Healthcare
items.reason: Hugo Bowne-Anderson interview with William Horton (Staff AI Engineer, Maven Clinic). Concrete case study: lead/specialist agent routing, 15-20 tools divided across specialists, deterministic + LLM-judge evals, release gating by consequence severity. Highly actionable for AI agent architecture wiki.
items.action: Create Maven Clinic entity page; update agent-architecture and agent-evaluation concept pages

items.subject: [AINews] not much happened today
items.reason: AINews daily digest from swyx (Latent Space). Title suggests a quiet day, but AINews often contains multiple small stories worth tracking. Requires fetching the actual post to identify specific items.
items.action: Fetch and review for any smaller stories; update AINews/swyx entity pages if needed

items.subject: AI Will Never Give the Same Answer Twice
items.reason: The Signal newsletter by Alex Banks. Philosophical piece on LLM non-determinism. General interest but likely lacks novel technical depth. Paywalled (freemail flag on link 19 suggests limited access).
items.action: Skip unless fetch reveals novel technical content
